w:r.w:t: Product Requirements Document · Tech Stack · Implementation Plan · Architecture
Rishi · Data Head, Infinitix Club, SNIST

Building a full-stack application requires expertise across frontend, backend, database design, and DevOps. This creates a high barrier for non-technical founders and students. Existing AI app builders (Emergent, v0.dev, Bolt) solve parts of this problem but operate as black boxes with no transparency into how the code is generated.

AgentForge is a multi-agent AI platform that takes a user's natural language prompt and autonomously generates, previews, and deploys a complete full-stack application — with full transparency into each agent's role and decision.
B.Tech / Engineering students building portfolio projects
Prompt engineers and AI enthusiasts exploring agentic systems
Natural language prompt input for app description
User feedback loop with up to 3 revision cycles
GitHub commit of approved generated code
UI/UX Designer agent with Figma MCP integration
DevOps agent for CI/CD and Docker
"As a student, I want to describe my app idea in plain English and receive a working, deployed full-stack application within minutes, so that I can focus on ideation rather than implementation."
Agent-first IDE, free preview, Gemini 3 runtime
Agent role definition, task delegation, team coordination
LLM provider abstraction, tool integration layer
Shared real-time context between agents (session state)
Persistent task logs, conversation history, revision records
Cross-session memory so agents remember previous builds
Runs Node.js in-browser, mounts generated files, live preview
Embeds WebContainer preview inside AgentForge UI
Playwright MCP, Context7 MCP, Filesystem MCP
GitHub MCP, Sentry MCP, Context7 MCP
PostgreSQL MCP, Supabase MCP, Filesystem MCP
GitHub MCP, Brave Search MCP, Apify MCP
Create repos, commit generated code, manage branches
Final production deployment of generated apps
Install CrewAI, LangGraph, LangChain, Redis, PostgreSQL dependencies
Configure Anthropic API key and test Claude Sonnet connection
Define Orchestrator agent with CrewAI role, backstory, and goal
Build prompt parser: extract app type, features, and tech preferences from user input
Build task planner: Orchestrator breaks prompt into structured tasks for each specialist agent
Test: Orchestrator correctly delegates 'build a task manager' into Frontend/Backend/DB tasks
Define Frontend agent with React/Next.js system prompt and MCP tool access
Define Backend agent with FastAPI system prompt and GitHub MCP access
Define Database agent with Prisma/PostgreSQL system prompt and DB MCP access
Set up Redis for shared context: agent output namespace isolation
Set up PostgreSQL for task logs and revision history
Wire LangGraph workflow: Orchestrator → DB agent → Backend agent → Frontend agent (sequential with parallel options)
Test: Full pipeline generates files for a simple CRUD app
Integrate @webcontainer/api into the AgentForge frontend UI
Mount generated files into WebContainer on completion
Render live preview inside React iframe component
Build feedback input UI alongside the preview
Wire feedback → Orchestrator → targeted agent re-run (only affected agent reruns)
Implement 3-loop revision cap with token cost guardrails
Test: User sees live app, gives feedback, preview updates within 60 seconds
Integrate PyGithub: auto-create repo, push generated code with semantic commit messages
Trigger Antigravity deployment on user approval
Add agent activity log panel: show which agent is running and what it produced
Prepare 3 demo prompts with recorded video backup
Performance testing: measure token cost and latency per app generation
User enters natural language prompt in AgentForge UI
Parses prompt via LLM API call, creates task plan, delegates to agents
Each agent makes its own LLM API call, generates code files
Agents write output to Redis namespace, read each other's context
Generated files mounted in-browser sandbox, live preview rendered
User reviews preview, gives feedback, targeted agent reruns (max 3x)
Approved code committed with semantic messages via GitHub API
Antigravity deploys the final app to production URL

Agents do not communicate directly. All inter-agent communication flows through the Orchestrator and shared Redis memory. Each agent writes to its own namespace (e.g. agent:frontend:output) and reads from others' namespaces as needed. This prevents race conditions and makes debugging straightforward via LangSmith traces.
API endpoints from Backend + design req
When a user provides feedback after viewing the preview:
Orchestrator makes one LLM call to classify which agent(s) need to rerun
Only the affected agent(s) rerun with the new context
Unaffected agents' output remains cached in Redis
Loop counter increments — hard cap at 3 revisions per session
Frontend + Backend + DB agents run in parallel where possible
Agents only spin up when their task is ready, not all at once
Claude Haiku for validation, summaries, and classification
Only affected agent reruns on feedback, not all 4
Hard cap on total tokens per session (configurable)
Agent outputs cached to avoid redundant LLM calls on re-render
Simpler role definition, better LangChain integration
Fast reads, persistent logs, namespace isolation
Better code gen, longer context, cost per token
The following are explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for v1.0 and will be tackled in v2.0:
Real-time features (WebSockets, SSE) — CRUD only in MVP
Complex authentication — basic JWT auth scaffold only
Database migrations that handle production data safely
Mobile app generation — web apps only
Multi-user collaboration on the same project
Security agent, Performance agent, QA agent, Docs agent
